disclaimer: Seed data compiled from public sources. Every row confirmed_by_counsel=false until ratified by immigration counsel. Not legal advice.

rules.value: April 1 of the fiscal year for which H-1B status is requested, or the validity start date of the approved petition, whichever is earlier
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A), as amended by 89 FR 103054 (Dec. 18, 2024) (H-1B modernization final rule)
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Extends both F-1 duration of status and any employment authorization granted under 8 CFR 274a.12(c)(3)(i)(B) or (C) (post-completion OPT and STEM OPT). First applied beginning with the FY 2026 H-1B cap season (registrations March 2025, petitions filed from April 1, 2025) per SEVP. Verified in force in current eCFR point-in-time text (2026-07-01).

rules.value: October 1 of the fiscal year for which H-1B status is requested (F-1 status extended until Oct 1; OPT work authorization through September 30)
rules.source_citation: 73 FR 18944 (Apr. 8, 2008) interim final rule; former 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED value. Cap-gap relief for all F-1 students with pending change-of-status H-1B petitions was established by the 2008 interim final rule and retained by the 2016 STEM OPT rule (81 FR 13040); end date moved from Oct 1 to April 1 effective Jan. 17, 2025.

rules.source_citation: SEVP blog, 'Recent H-1B Rule Extends F-1 Cap-Gap Extension' (Apr. 28, 2025); 89 FR 103054
rules.notes: Up to approximately six additional months of F-1 status and work authorization (Oct 1 -> Apr 1) for students whose H-1B petitions remain pending or whose approved petitions have deferred start dates.

rules.value: Automatic upon proper, timely filing of a nonfrivolous cap-subject H-1B petition requesting change of status; no student application, no fee, no new EAD issued
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A); USCIS cap-gap page
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Mechanism originally established Apr. 8, 2008; current text requires the petition to be (1) timely filed, (2) requesting an H-1B employment start date in the fiscal year for which status is requested, and (3) nonfrivolous.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A)-(B); USCIS cap-gap page
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Petition must indicate change of status, not consular processing. Cap-gap terminates upon denial or withdrawal of the COS request even if the H-1B petition itself is approved for consular processing (8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(B)).

rules.source_citation: SEVP SEVIS Help Hub, F-1 Cap Gap Extension (last updated Apr. 10, 2025); USCIS cap-gap page ('the cap-gap provision applies only to beneficiaries of cap-subject H-1B petitions')
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Petitions by cap-exempt employers (institutions of higher education, nonprofit research organizations, government research organizations) are not eligible; such employers may file H-1B petitions year-round, so no cap-gap arises.

rules.value: Filed during the applicable H-1B filing period (begins April 1) while the student's authorized F-1 duration-of-status admission is still in effect - during the academic course of study, any authorized period of post-completion OPT or STEM OPT, or the 60-day grace period - and based on a valid, selected registration for the same beneficiary and fiscal year (unless the registration requirement is suspended)
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A)(1); USCIS cap-gap page
rules.notes: For extension of EMPLOYMENT AUTHORIZATION (as opposed to status only), M-274 adds that the petition must also have been properly filed before the expiration date of the OPT EAD.

rules.value: Both F-1 status AND OPT/STEM OPT work authorization extend only if the student is in an authorized period of post-completion OPT or STEM OPT (unexpired EAD) on the date the H-1B petition is filed; if the student is in F-1 status but not on OPT, only status extends
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A); 8 CFR 274a.12(b)(6)(v); USCIS Handbook for Employers M-274 sec. 7.4.2 (updated Apr. 2, 2025)
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. M-274 describes the two cap-gap types: extension of F-1 status only (no OPT at filing - work authorized only once H-1B is approved and takes effect) vs. extension of status plus OPT (work authorized through March 31/April 1 or petition validity start date, whichever earlier).

rules.source_citation: USCIS cap-gap page ('If an H-1B cap-subject petition is properly filed for a student who has entered the 60-day grace period, the student will receive the automatic extension of their F-1 status, but will not be authorized to work')
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Filing during the 60-day grace period extends status only; the student was not work-authorized at filing and remains not work-authorized until the H-1B takes effect.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A) (referencing employment authorization under 8 CFR 274a.12(c)(3)(i)(B) or (C)); SEVP 'H-1B Status and the Cap Gap Extension' page
rules.notes: Students in an authorized STEM OPT extension period at filing receive the same automatic extension of status and work authorization. 24-month STEM OPT framework effective May 10, 2016 (81 FR 13040); cap-gap coverage of STEM OPT dates to the 2008 IFR (17-month era).

rules.source_citation: SEVP Policy Guidance 1004-03 sec. 9.4.3 ('the 90 day limitation on unemployment continues during the cap gap extension') and sec. 7.1.1
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Unemployment days continue to accrue during cap-gap OPT and count against the aggregate limits in 8 CFR 214.2(f)(10)(ii)(E); no additional unemployment days are granted for the cap-gap period. Exceeding the limit during cap-gap is a status violation.

Aggregate calendar days of unemployment permitted during any post-completion OPT period, including any cap-gap extension of that OPT. Renamed from opt-unemployment-limit-days to avoid rule_id collision with the canonical row in rules_f1.json.

Aggregate limit across the total OPT period (post-completion OPT plus 24-month STEM extension), including any cap-gap extension. Replaced the 120-day aggregate limit that applied under the 2008 17-month STEM rule. Renamed from stem-opt-unemployment-limit-days to avoid rule_id collision with the canonical row in rules_f1.json.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(iv); see also 8 CFR 214.2(f)(10)(ii) (D/S for OPT students ends 60 days after OPT employment authorization expires)
rules.notes: Departure-preparation ('grace') period after completing the course of study or authorized practical training. Longstanding provision; original promulgation date not pinned to a primary source. WATCH: a DHS final rule ending duration of status (which as proposed would cut this to 30 days) cleared OIRA review June 17, 2026 but had NOT been published in the Federal Register as of 2026-07-06, so 60 days remains in force.

rules.value: Automatic termination upon rejection, denial, revocation, or withdrawal of the H-1B petition, or upon denial or withdrawal of the change-of-status request (even if the H-1B petition is approved for consular processing); non-selection in the registration lottery also ends eligibility; if approved, cap-gap ends on the petition's validity start date
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(B); USCIS cap-gap page
rules.notes: On denial/rejection/revocation/withdrawal, SEVIS reverts the F-1 record to the original program/OPT end date and removes the cap-gap annotation from the Form I-20.

rules.source_citation: USCIS cap-gap page ('the student will have the standard 60-day grace period from the date of the notification of the denial, rejection, revocation, or withdrawal of the petition to depart the United States')
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Runs from the date the extension of status terminated (notification date) or the program/OPT end date, whichever is later. Departure preparation only - no work authorization during this period.

rules.source_citation: USCIS cap-gap page ('The 60-day grace period does not apply to an F-1 student whose accompanying change of status request is denied due to a status violation, misrepresentation, or fraud... students are required to immediately leave the United States')
rules.notes: Confirms hypothesis. Also applies where the petition is revoked based on a finding of status violation, fraud, or misrepresentation discovered after approval: no cap-gap, no grace period, immediate departure required.
To receive the automatic extension of stay and employment authorization, the student must not have violated the terms or conditions of their nonimmigrant status (consistent with 8 CFR part 248).
The automatic extension of the F-1 student's duration of status also applies to the duration of status of any F-2 dependents.

rules.value: Employee's expired OPT/STEM OPT EAD presented together with the employer's Form I-797C receipt notice for the timely filed cap-subject H-1B change-of-status petition constitutes an unexpired List A EAD. Record: Document Title 'EAD'; Document Number = Form I-797C receipt number; Expiration Date = March 31 of the fiscal year of the H-1B employment start date; 'CAP-GAP' in Additional Information
rules.source_citation: USCIS Handbook for Employers M-274 sec. 7.4.2 (Cap-Gap; last reviewed Apr. 2, 2025); 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(B)
rules.notes: CORRECTS hypothesis: current M-274 keys the List A combination to the expired EAD plus Form I-797C, not the cap-gap Form I-20. The DSO-issued cap-gap I-20 remains valid proof of continued status/OPT (see capgap-i20-role) but is not the I-9 document. In Section 1 the new employee selects 'alien authorized to work until' March 31 of the relevant fiscal year.

rules.value: Employee's expired OPT/STEM OPT EAD presented together with the employer's Form I-797C receipt notice for a timely filed H-1B change-of-status petition stating an Oct. 1 employment start date constituted an unexpired List A EAD. Record: Document Title 'EAD'; Document Number = Form I-797C receipt number; Expiration Date = Sept. 30 of the year the petition was filed; 'CAP-GAP' in Additional Information; reverification by Sept. 30
rules.source_citation: USCIS Handbook for Employers M-274 sec. 7.4.2, pre-2025 edition (Internet Archive snapshot of May 20, 2024)
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED procedure retained for interpreting historical I-9s. CORRECTED 2026-07-06 against the archived May 20, 2024 M-274 edition: the pre-2025 List A combination was ALSO expired EAD + Form I-797C (not expired EAD + DSO-endorsed cap-gap Form I-20 as previously recorded); only the dates differed (Sept. 30 expiration/reverification, petition required to state an Oct. 1 start date). Section 1: 'A noncitizen authorized to work until' Sept. 30 of the filing year. UNVERIFIED: whether still-earlier M-274 editions (pre-2023) keyed the combination to a DSO-endorsed cap-gap I-20; effective_date left null because the promulgation date of this M-274 text was not established.

rules.value: On or by March 31 of the relevant fiscal year (Form I-9 Supplement B, Reverification and Rehire); upon H-1B approval, reverify using the new Form I-94 issued with the approval Form I-797, or any other acceptable List A or C document or receipt
rules.source_citation: USCIS Handbook for Employers M-274 sec. 7.4.2, Cap-Gap Reverification
rules.notes: For a current employee, once the Form I-797C is received the employer enters 'CAP-GAP' and 'March 31' plus the fiscal year in the Additional Information field of Section 2 (e.g., CAP-GAP 03/31/yyyy). E-Verify employers may create a case using the Form I-9 information.

rules.value: SEVIS automatically adds the cap-gap extension when USCIS CLAIMS data shows a pending or approved eligible petition; the DSO issues an updated Form I-20 showing the extension (to April 1). The I-20 is proof of continued F-1 status and OPT but is NOT required to continue working - the extension is automatic. If SEVIS is not auto-updated, the DSO may request an SEVP Response Center data fix based on the Form I-797 receipt notice
rules.source_citation: SEVP SEVIS Help Hub, F-1 Cap Gap Extension (updated Apr. 10, 2025); USCIS cap-gap page, 'Proof of Continuing Status'
rules.notes: USCIS: student may first obtain an interim updated I-20 based on evidence of filing (petition copy plus mailing receipt), then a further updated I-20 showing extension until April 1 once the Form I-797 receipt notice with a valid receipt number is presented.

rules.source_citation: USCIS cap-gap page ('A student does not file an application for the extension or receive a new Employment Authorization Document (EAD) to cover the additional time')
rules.notes: USCIS will not renew the EAD card for the cap-gap interim period; the existing (expired) EAD plus I-797C/I-20 documents the extension.

rules.value: Departing the United States while the H-1B change-of-status petition is pending causes the COS request to be deemed abandoned; the student cannot re-enter under cap-gap. After the petition AND COS are approved, the student may travel and seek readmission in F-1 status before the H-1B effective date (normally Oct 1) if otherwise admissible; no valid EAD is required at reentry
rules.source_citation: USCIS cap-gap page, 'Travel during Cap-Gap Extension Period'; SEVP 'H-1B Status and the Cap Gap Extension' page
rules.notes: CBP makes the final admission determination at the port of entry.

rules.value: April 1 preceding the October 1 fiscal-year start; a petition may not be filed more than 6 months before the date of actual need for the beneficiary's services
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(2)(i) (time-of-filing, 6-month advance-filing limit); USCIS cap-gap page
rules.notes: This 6-month limit is why the cap-gap exists. Note: the (f)(5)(vi)(A)(2) cross-reference to '(h)(2)(i)(I)' predates a concurrent H-2 rule that redesignated the time-of-filing provision to (h)(2)(i)(J) in the current CFR text.

rules.value: Petition must request an H-1B employment start date within the fiscal year for which H-1B status is requested; since Jan. 17, 2025 the requested start date need not be exactly October 1 - SEVP treats petitions requesting start dates between October 1 and April 1 of the following year as cap-gap eligible
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A)(2); 89 FR 103054; SEVP SEVIS Help Hub, F-1 Cap Gap Extension
rules.notes: SEVIS eligibility also requires the student's program/OPT/STEM OPT end date to fall after the petition filing date and between April 1 of the current year and April 1 of the following year.

rules.value: October 1 (earliest date an approved cap-subject H-1B change of status takes effect)
rules.source_citation: USCIS cap-gap page; INA 214(g) (fiscal-year numerical limitations)
rules.notes: The federal fiscal year runs October 1 - September 30. Under the 2025 modernization rule an approved COS may take effect on a requested start date after Oct 1 within the fiscal year.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(h)(8)(iii)(D)(3) ('The filing period for filing the H-1B cap-subject petition will be at least 90 days'), added by 84 FR 888 (Jan. 31, 2019, effective Apr. 1, 2019); SEVP SEVIS Help Hub ('Selected employers have a 90-day window to file the selected petitions')
rules.notes: CORRECTED 2026-07-06: the at-least-90-day filing window was set by the H-1B registration final rule, 84 FR 888 (effective Apr. 1, 2019) - the NPRM had proposed at least 60 days and DHS raised it to 90 in the final rule; electronic registration was first used for the FY2021 cap season (registration March 2020). Verified in current eCFR (h)(8)(iii)(D)(3). Registration selection occurs by March 31; petitions may be filed in the designated at-least-90-day window. SEVP warns the filing window can fall after a student's OPT (and even grace period) ends, in which case no cap-gap work authorization (or extension at all) is possible.

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (Jan. 31, 2024) USCIS fee schedule final rule
rules.notes: Per-beneficiary H-1B electronic registration fee; raised from $10 by the April 2024 fee rule (89 FR 6194, rule effective 2024-04-01) and first applied to the FY2026 registration period; effective_date follows the seed convention of the first registration-period date the fee applied (FY2026 initial registration opened 2025-03-07). Verified unchanged at $215 for the FY2027 registration period (March 4-19, 2026) per USCIS FY2027 cap registration alert. Relevant because cap-gap eligibility requires the petition be based on a valid, selected registration.

rules.source_citation: INA 214(g)(1)(A); USCIS H-1B Cap Season page
rules.notes: Statutory annual cap defining 'cap-subject'; reverted to 65,000 beginning FY2004. Up to 6,800 set aside for H-1B1 (Chile/Singapore).

rules.source_citation: INA 214(g)(5)(C); USCIS H-1B Cap Season page
rules.notes: Annual exemption for beneficiaries with a U.S. master's degree or higher (H-1B Visa Reform Act of 2004). Petitions counted under this exemption are still cap-subject for cap-gap purposes.

rules.value: In force. The H-1B modernization final rule (89 FR 103054, effective Jan. 17, 2025), including the April 1 cap-gap extension, is codified in the current eCFR (point-in-time text retrieved 2026-07-01); no injunction, vacatur, or rescission affecting the cap-gap provision was found as of 2026-07-06
rules.notes: Re-verified 2026-07-06: current eCFR (f)(5)(vi) retains the April 1 text; USCIS cap-gap page (updated 01/17/2025) and SEVP SEVIS Help Hub (updated 04/10/2025) remain live with the April 1 rule; litigation trackers show no challenge to the modernization rule or its cap-gap provision. 2025-2026 H-1B litigation targets other policies: the $100,000 proclamation fee was vacated June 8, 2026 in State of California v. Mullin, No. 25-13829-LTS (D. Mass., Sorokin, J.) (also reported as California v. Noem/Trump), partially stayed by the district court June 12, 2026, with appeal pending in the First Circuit (notice of appeal June 11, 2026; stay motion filed with the First Circuit June 18, 2026); that fee applies to consular-processed petitions and generally does not reach in-US change-of-status cap petitions. WATCH: the DHS final rule ending F/J/I duration of status cleared OIRA June 17, 2026 but was not published in the Federal Register as of 2026-07-06.

rules.value: Approved change of status takes effect on the validity start date of the approved H-1B petition (earliest Oct 1; may be later within the fiscal year); cap-gap F-1 status and any cap-gap OPT end on that date
rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(vi)(A); USCIS cap-gap page
rules.notes: If the extension runs to April 1 with the petition still pending, work authorization ends March 31 and the employer must reverify; the student retains F-1 status protection only per the terms of the pending COS adjudication - USCIS guidance directs reverification by March 31.

rules.source_citation: SEVP SEVIS Help Hub, F-1 Cap Gap Extension, 'Correcting Cap Gap Information in SEVIS'
rules.notes: If the USCIS CLAIMS interface does not auto-add the cap-gap to the SEVIS record, the DSO should wait two weeks after USCIS confirms timely receipt, then request a data fix from the SEVP Response Center (sevp@ice.dhs.gov / 1-800-892-4829) with a copy of the Form I-797 receipt notice.

rules.value: Students on cap-gap OPT remain subject to standard OPT reporting: report changes of address, legal name, employer, and interruptions of employment within 10 days; unemployment must stay within the 90/150-day aggregate limits
rules.source_citation: SEVP Policy Guidance 1004-03 sec. 9.4.4; 8 CFR 214.2(f)(12), (17)
rules.notes: Failure to report or exceeding unemployment limits during cap-gap jeopardizes the F-1 status underlying the pending change of status.
